title: Chersodoma arequipensis
taxonomy: Chersodoma arequipensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae, commonly known as the verbena family. This family of plants is a group of dicotyledonous flowering plants. The genus Chersodoma is native to South America, particularly in the Andes mountain range. It belongs to the order Lamiales, which consists of plants with bilaterally symmetrical flowers and a 4-5 parted corolla. Chersodoma arequipensis is further categorized as a member of the tribe Verbenaceae tribe, Veroniceae.

intro: Chersodoma arequipensis, also known as the 'Yareta', is a succulent, shrub-like plant native to the high-altitude Andean regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Chersodoma arequipensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
